draft.title: ai workspace
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
ai workspace

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: The user may prefer interfaces that feel inviting and human-centered rather than clinical or stark
facade.label: Warm glow
facade.acceptImplies: Explore softer color palettes, ambient lighting effects, rounded corners, and tactile material cues
facade.rejectImplies: Consider cooler tones, high contrast, sharp geometry, and minimal ornamentation for the workspace aesthetic

evidence.hypothesis: The user may prefer interfaces that feel inviting and human-centered rather than clinical or stark
evidence.implication: Explore softer color palettes, ambient lighting effects, rounded corners, and tactile material cues

facade.hypothesis: Smooth, spreading feedback on interaction feels more natural than instant state changes
facade.label: Hover ripples
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers motion and animation to acknowledge their gestures; workspace should reward interaction with graceful feedback
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers immediate, zero-latency response; motion is noise; interaction should feel direct and frictionless

facade.hypothesis: AI workspaces may favor high-contrast dark interfaces to reduce eye strain during long focus sessions and signal 'technical' or 'serious' work mode
facade.label: Dark background, light text
facade.acceptImplies: User leans toward dark/night mode aesthetics; values reduced brightness and technical appearance
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers bright, airy, or neutral backgrounds; may want warmth or approachability in their workspace UI

facade.hypothesis: Users building an AI workspace prefer conversational, approachable tone over corporate formality
facade.label: Casual and direct
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into warm, human language. Use contractions. Speak like a knowledgeable friend, not a manual.
facade.rejectImplies: Shift toward polished, formal, trust-signaling language. Use complete sentences. Adopt enterprise tone.

facade.hypothesis: For AI workspaces, users prefer seeing key metrics (tokens used, API calls, cost) as large, scannable cards over dense data tables or narrative explanations.
facade.axisTargeted: Dense data vs. key metrics — whether to lead with headline numbers or detailed breakdowns
facade.label: Dashboard with big numbers
facade.acceptImplies: Emphasize glanceable stat cards, prominent KPIs, minimalist metric display; surface what matters most immediately
facade.rejectImplies: Lean into detailed tables, full context on every metric, narrative explanations alongside numbers; make all data equally visible

facade.hypothesis: A vertical sidebar navigation paired with card-based content in the main area provides better task scoping and content organization than full-width layouts for knowledge work
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation architecture + Content grouping (sidebar vs full-width; discrete units vs continuous flow)
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values clear task compartmentalization and appreciates persistent navigation anchors; may prefer depth-first exploration patterns
facade.rejectImplies: User wants maximum working canvas and fewer visual divisions; prefers tab-based or full-width approaches; values continuous scanning over hierarchical drilling

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface layout
Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs neutral/professional communication style
Whether user wants a calm/focused atmosphere vs energetic/stimulating workspace mood
Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards vs simple, task-focused information display
Whether user likes smooth/animated transitions vs instant/snappy interface responses

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface layout
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs neutral/professional communication style
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants a calm/focused atmosphere vs energetic/stimulating workspace mood
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards vs simple, task-focused information display
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user likes smooth/animated transitions vs instant/snappy interface responses
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: The user may want their AI workspace to feel less isolating and more contextually rich, with subtle environmental cues (sound, micro-interactions) that create presence without distraction
facade.axisTargeted: Organic vs Digital — testing whether sterile silence feels right or if gentle, nature-inspired sensory layers would enhance focus
facade.label: Ambient soundscape
facade.acceptImplies: Explore subtle audio feedback, environmental ambience options, nature sounds, or gentle notification tones that humanize the digital experience
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers clean silence and pure visual focus; audio elements may feel gimmicky or intrusive to their workflow

draft.title: AI Workspace Hub
draft.summary: A focused workspace interface for managing AI tasks, projects, and collaborative workflows. Clean, minimal design with warm neutrals and intuitive navigation.
